title: FRMI News & Price Impact
markdown:
# Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ: FRMI) — News & Price-Impact Summary

**Window:** last 14 days (through 2026-07-07) · **Price:** ~$7.46 (-9.1% on the day; faded from ~$8.6 in early July after a strong June)
**Net read:** Mixed, leaning cautious near-term. The governance overhang that had been feeding the rally resolved in the *board's* favor today — the founder's proxy campaign collapsed and shareholders rejected his special-meeting bid — knocking the stock ~9%. The genuine bull case (a hyperscaler leasing Project Matador's behind-the-meter power) is still intact but remains **unsigned and speculative**. This is a pre-revenue, deeply loss-making, extraordinarily volatile story whose price is a bet on landing a marquee AI tenant, not on current fundamentals.

## What's moving the stock

Two forces have been fighting over FRMI for the past few weeks, and this week one of them broke.

The first is a **corporate-governance war** between Fermi's founder and largest shareholder, former CEO **Toby Neugebauer**, and the company's current board (whose co-founders include former Texas Governor Rick Perry). Neugebauer has argued the board is mismanaging the company's single crown-jewel asset — **Project Matador**, a ~7,500-acre AI-datacenter-plus-power campus in Amarillo, Texas — by refusing to "market test" it. Through a series of dissident proxy filings (DFAN14A), he pushed to call a **special shareholder meeting** to replace a majority of the board and launch an independent, banker-led **"dual-track" strategic review** — i.e., put the platform up for a sale-versus-lease auction over roughly 75 days to crystallize its value. His pitch framed Matador as a scarce, partly-capitalized asset (~2+ GW of secured generation, a ~6 GW clean-air permit, >$1B of financing arranged) whose four big risks — lease pricing, financing, dilution, and counterparty/execution — justify letting bankers run a process now. Many traders liked this because a forced sale or auction is a near-term way to "unlock" value.

That thesis unraveled over the past week. On **July 3**, Neugebauer **suspended his proxy campaign** after the presiding Texas Business Court judge abruptly recused himself just before a key hearing, blowing up his timeline; he claimed ~70% of recorded votes had favored holding the special meeting and said he'd keep fighting through the courts (including a challenge to a board bylaw change raising the supermajority threshold to 70%). Then **today, July 7**, the company declared victory — "Fermi Shareholders Send Clear Message They Do Not Support Former CEO's Special Meeting Proposal" — clearing the way to proceed with the board's own plan. **The stock fell ~9% on the news.** The read: with the dissident sidelined, the market lost the near-term optionality of a banker-run sale process, and the governance uncertainty that had *added* volatility (and speculative upside) drained out. It's a stock-specific move tied directly to the dated headlines, not a market-wide day.

The second force — and the real long-term driver — is **AI-datacenter power demand**. Fermi's model is behind-the-meter gigawatt-scale private power for hyperscalers who can't get grid interconnection fast enough. Through June the stock ran hard (up ~50% over five days at one point, then +20-26% on June 12) after analysts at **JMP/Citizens** reported that **OpenAI** is likely among several counterparties assessing capacity at Project Matador, tied to broader reporting that OpenAI is negotiating a ~10 GW data-center lease (with Nvidia funding) — bullish for anyone with "power-ready" campuses. Fermi is targeting ~1.1 GW of new capacity by end-2026. **But no tenant has actually been signed.** Neugebauer himself said on June 24 he was "highly confident a tenant will be announced soon" — confidence, not a contract. Until a hyperscaler signs, this is an anticipated catalyst, and the whole valuation debate (the stock at ~$7.46 versus third-party "fair value" estimates of $19-23) hangs on it.

Net: the governance chapter just closed in the board's favor (near-term negative for the trade), while the tenant catalyst that could re-rate the stock is still pending. Hence today's drop despite an otherwise strong month.

## Other developments

**EPC contract awards signal execution progress.** On **June 30**, Fermi selected **Primoris Services** to engineer and construct the balance-of-plant for six SGT-800 gas turbines in Matador phase one; on **June 25** it engaged **TSK** (Spain's largest EPC firm) for early-works engineering on three Siemens SGT6-5000F turbines in phase two. These are mildly bullish — they show the power build-out is actually being contracted, not just announced — but they're incremental spend, not revenue, and didn't move the stock much against the louder governance headlines.
— StockTitan (Tier 1/company PR) · 2026-06-30 / 2026-06-25 · product/contract · bullish (low conf.) · noise-to-low impact

**Stifel cut its price target to $17 from $29** (~June 23), and options activity around that time was flagged as heavy and directionally bearish. Even after the cut, $17 sits well above the current ~$7.46, and the consensus is still nominally "Buy" (avg target ~$19) — but the cut reflects the same uncertainty (financing, dilution, unsigned leases) the bulls are waving away.
— stockanalysis.com / Nasdaq (Tier 1/2) · ~2026-06-23 · analyst rating · bearish (medium) · contributed to the mid-window pullback

**Water-stewardship positioning** (June 24): Fermi told Texas lawmakers Matador will use "80% less water" than conventional datacenter cooling via recycling and hybrid systems — a permitting/political-goodwill move in drought-sensitive West Texas, not a price catalyst. Noise for trading purposes.

**Filtered as noise:** the usual "Is FRMI undervalued / should you buy" pieces (Simply Wall St, Motley Fool-style listicles) and recycled quote-page reprints. Seen and set aside.

## Quick reference

| Date | Headline | Impact |
|------|----------|--------|
| 2026-07-07 | Shareholders reject former CEO's special-meeting proposal; board proceeds | CONFIRMED −9.1% |
| 2026-07-03 | Neugebauer suspends proxy campaign after judge's recusal (claims ~70% of votes) | CONFIRMED (part of the multi-day governance de-rating) |
| 2026-07-01 | Neugebauer: "What is the board afraid to say under oath?" (litigation) | noise / low |
| 2026-06-30 | Selects Primoris for balance-of-plant, 6 SGT-800 turbines (phase 1) | ANTICIPATED / low |
| 2026-06-25 | Selects TSK (Spain EPC) for phase-2 early works | ANTICIPATED / low |
| 2026-06-24 | Neugebauer "highly confident tenant announced soon" | ANTICIPATED (unsigned) |
| ~2026-06-23 | Stifel cuts PT to $17 from $29 | CONFIRMED-ish (bearish drag) |
| 2026-06-12 | JMP/Citizens: OpenAI among parties eyeing Matador capacity | CONFIRMED +20-26% (pre-window run-up) |

## What to watch

Near-term the trade is mixed-to-cautious: the governance catalyst is spent (board won), and the stock gave back ground today, so the next real mover is a **signed anchor tenant** — that's the single most important thing to watch, because the entire valuation gap ($7.46 vs. $17-23 targets) rests on converting "OpenAI/hyperscaler is looking" into an executed lease.

- **A signed Project Matador lease / tenant announcement** — timing unknown; management hints "soon." This is the binary re-rating event.
- **Q2 earnings — expected 2026-07-28** (consensus EPS ~−$0.06). Watch cash burn, financing/dilution, and capacity-delivery timeline; last year's FY2025 GAAP loss was ~$486M (mostly non-cash).
- **The Neugebauer litigation** — he's pursuing governance changes and the 70%-supermajority bylaw challenge through the Texas courts; a ruling could revive the sale-process narrative.
- **AI-power sentiment** (OpenAI/Nvidia capacity headlines) — FRMI trades as a high-beta proxy for hyperscaler power demand.

*Not investment advice. Price attribution is inferential — it links headlines to observed moves but cannot prove causation. Price data is end-of-day/delayed; verify anything you act on against primary sources (SEC EDGAR, company IR).*
